summary: base58-core ships a crypto-clipper, clipboard exfiltration, and shell/login persistence
details: On require(), base58-core installs a time-delayed (72h) crypto-clipper that polls the OS clipboard every 2.5 seconds, detects BTC/ETH/SOL addresses, seed phrases, WIF keys, and 64-hex private keys, and rewrites the clipboard to hardcoded attacker wallets (BTC bc1qjft978uykglsh0adcyx6xhkes56vqzs3fual3l, ETH 0xd63eD44065eDb1e2ad2519B011c06412dA7B7c5B, SOL A7ajd7W5WYdrnkeaiBRjVoK6uBEDvgnuZcpzQXqo18Ph), hijacking outgoing transfers. Matched clipboard contents along with hostname, platform, and cwd are POSTed as JSON to a hardcoded bare IP over plaintext HTTP at http://2.27.62.51:8080/api/health (backup http://2.27.62.51:8081/api/health). The package establishes persistence by appending a hook to ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, and ~/.profile that respawns the payload via `node -e require('@base58/core')._internal.activate()`, and on Windows writes base58-runtime.js into the Startup folder, so the clipper survives shell relogin and OS restart. PowerShell is invoked via child_process.execSync to interact with the Windows clipboard. The 72-hour activation delay is an evasion control intended to bypass install-time sandboxes and ephemeral CI. The package name and keywords impersonate the legitimate base58 encoder ecosystem.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
